findings.title: New URMA failover flag validators lack negative coverage
findings.impact: The PR adds acceptance boundaries for the hot-updated success-rate ratio and minimum sample count, but the added UT/ST only use valid values. A regression that accepts NaN, values above 1.0, negative ratios, or min_sample_count=0 would not be caught, allowing invalid client config to disable or over-trigger failover at runtime.
findings.fix: Add a flags/validator test using the repository flag parsing path, for example SetCommandLineOption or client config reload, that rejects ratio values outside [0.0, 1.0] including non-finite input and rejects urma_failover_min_sample_count=0 while preserving the previous valid value.

findings.title: Stale URMA failover callback branch has no regression test
findings.impact: SubmitUrmaDataPlaneSwitch now has a branch for an async URMA failure callback whose worker is no longer current. None of the added STs force a worker switch between callback submission and execution, so a regression that fails to call FinishUrmaDataPlaneSwitchAttempt(false) on the stale path could leave switchInProgress stuck and suppress all later URMA failover attempts.
findings.fix: Add a focused test or inject-driven ST that delays the async switch task, changes the current worker before the task runs, then verifies the stale callback is ignored and the tracker accepts a later unhealthy window by triggering a new switch.
